plainEnglish: Navigation that repeats across pages—main menu, search, breadcrumbs—must appear in the same relative order on every page. Users learn where things are; shuffling the navigation order between pages forces them to relearn it each time.
whyItMatters: Screen reader users and people with cognitive disabilities rely on navigation being in a predictable place; reordering it between pages forces them to re-find everything.
quickCheck: Compare several pages and confirm repeated navigation items appear in the same relative order on each.
commonMistakes: Reordering menu items differently from page to page.
Moving the search box or nav to a different position on some pages.
Conditionally hiding or reordering nav links inconsistently across templates.

codeExamples.label: One shared Nav renders the same order everywhere
codeExamples.code:
{/* A single shared component keeps order consistent across pages */}
<Layout>
  <Nav />
  <main>{children}</main>
</Layout>

codeExamples.label: Navigation order differs per page
codeExamples.code:
{/* Home lists Products first; About lists Contact first */}
<nav>{page === "home" ? <Products /> : <Contact />}</nav>
